title: Inference economics matrix — $/1k-token, latency, tokens/sec
description: Reproducible per-endpoint inference-economics measurements produced by src/inference-bench.js against an OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint, with and without speculative decoding. Every row carries a content digest (and, when a signing key is configured, a detached ed25519 signature) so the numbers can be re-verified off the wire. Re-generate a row with: kolm bench inference --endpoint <url> --model <id> --speculative auto. Do NOT hand-edit measured rows.

units.mean_ms: end-to-end per-call latency, milliseconds (includes any proxy hop)
units.cost_per_1k_usd: blended USD to produce 1000 tokens (input+output) at the supplied published per-token prices; 0 when prices are not supplied
units.throughput_speedup: with-speculative tokens_per_sec / baseline tokens_per_sec; null unless BOTH arms ran error-free
units.latency_speedup: baseline mean_ms / with-speculative mean_ms; null unless BOTH arms ran error-free

caveats: tokens_per_sec is single-stream (concurrency=1) by construction — it is not an aggregate-throughput claim. Re-run with a load generator for batched-throughput numbers.
speculative_speedup is only populated when both the baseline and the with-speculative arms actually ran against the live endpoint. A null speedup means it was not measured, never that there is none.
cost_per_1k_usd reflects the per-token prices passed to the runner; for local hardware it is 0 unless an amortized per-token price is supplied.

the with-speculative arm sends the draft-model hint the OpenAI-compatible route accepts (vLLM/SGLang extra_body). A server that ignores the hint will produce numbers indistinguishable from baseline — confirm acceptance in the server logs before citing a speedup.
rows marked dry_run:true are deterministic offline stubs (no network, no API key) used to exercise the schema in CI; they are NOT measurements.
